title: angelica polymorpha
intro: Angelica polymorpha, also known as American angelica or wild angelica, is a perennial plant that belongs to the carrot family and is native to North America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of angelica polymorpha include relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis, coughs, and colds, as well as its ability to reduce fever and alleviate menstrual cramps. Angelica polymorpha can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and its roots can also be dried and used in herbal remedies.
